commitLog.message: feat: add Groq as primary LLM provider with multi-provider support
fix: correct directory structure and update to Next.js 16 with Python 3.13 support
feat: complete project setup — Next.js 16, Python 3.13, Groq integration
feat(ai-worker): implement core AI worker with BANT qualification engine
feat(ai-worker): implement SOLID principles, JWT auth, and streaming responses
feat(maestro): implement core backend — domain, repositories, middleware, server
feat(auth): implement frontend auth flow and harden maestro security

phase1.name: MVP e Validação de Fluxo
phase1.description: Core backend, AI qualification engine, and minimal chat frontend — the full end-to-end BANT flow running in production.

steps.name: Project Setup & Infrastructure
steps.description: Monorepo structure, Docker Compose stack, environment config, database init script, Makefile automation.

subTasks.name: Monorepo directory structure (maestro, ai-worker, frontend)
Docker Compose — postgres, redis, qdrant, minio, maestro, ai-worker, frontend
PostgreSQL init script with RLS, extensions, seed data
Makefile with 70+ targets (dev, test, build, lint, deploy)
.env.example with all env vars documented
Optional monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana via profile)
Postman API collection

steps.name: Maestro — Domain Layer
steps.description: Go domain entities (Lead, Conversation, Message, Tenant, User), validation helpers, custom error types, repository port interfaces.

subTasks.name: Lead entity with BANT score, status enum, Validate()
Conversation entity with status machine, Validate()
Message entity with role enum, content-length cap, Validate()
Tenant entity with config JSONB, domain normalisation, Validate()
User entity with role enum
Custom error types: ErrNotFound, ErrValidation, ErrForbidden, ErrConflict
Repository port interfaces (ILeadRepository, IConversationRepository, IMessageRepository, ITenantRepository, IUserRepository)

steps.name: Maestro — Infrastructure & Repository Layer
steps.description: PostgreSQL connection with retry, GORM-based repositories, tenant context isolation, database migrations.

subTasks.name: PostgreSQL connection with 5-attempt linear backoff retry
Connection pool tuning (max open/idle, ConnMaxLifetime, ConnMaxIdleTime)
Schema migrations (9 migration files)
LeadRepository — CRUD with tenant isolation, pagination cap, UpdateQualification split
ConversationRepository — CRUD with cross-tenant LeadID ownership check
MessageRepository — CRUD with 100-message cap, IDOR guards on read and insert
TenantRepository — CRUD with unique-constraint conflict mapping
UserRepository — CRUD with bcrypt password hashing
tenantIDFromContext returning ErrForbidden on missing tenant

steps.name: Maestro — HTTP Server & Security Middleware
steps.description: Gin HTTP server with timeouts, rate limiting, body size cap, JWT auth, CORS, security headers, and structured logging.

subTasks.name: Gin server with read/write/idle timeouts (Slowloris protection)
Token-bucket rate limiter (per-IP, RWMutex, 100K cap, auto-eviction)
X-Forwarded-For spoofing protection — CIDR width validation on TrustedProxies
Request body size middleware (1 MB default, configurable)
Custom request logger with sensitive param redaction (case-insensitive)
JWT authentication middleware with role enum validation and JTI
CORS middleware via gin-contrib/cors with allowed origins
Security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy)
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
Health check endpoint (no env disclosure)
Config validation on startup (JWT_SECRET min 32 chars, APP_ENV enum, port range)

steps.name: AI Worker — Core Engine
steps.description: Python FastAPI service with Groq LLM integration, BANT scoring, conversation history, streaming SSE.

subTasks.name: FastAPI application with middleware and route registration
Groq LLM client with streaming SSE responses
BANT scoring algorithm (Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline)
Prompt templates for BANT qualification agent
Redis-based conversation history management
Pydantic request/response schema validation
Health check endpoint
Qdrant vector DB integration for RAG
subTasks.notes: Client wired, ingestion pipeline not yet implemented (tracked in p1-s9)

steps.name: AI Worker — Security & SOLID Refactor
steps.description: JWT authentication middleware, per-tenant rate limiting, SOLID principles applied across all layers.

subTasks.name: JWT token verification middleware (pyjwt)
Tenant context extraction from JWT claims
Per-tenant rate limiting middleware
Dependency injection for LLM client and scorer
Service layer separation (ChatService, ScorerService)
Structured logging with structlog
Full multi-provider fallback (Groq → OpenAI → Anthropic)
subTasks.notes: Groq primary only; fallback interface defined but routing logic pending

steps.name: Maestro — HTTP API Handlers (Leads & Conversations)
steps.description: HTTP handler implementations for the lead and conversation REST endpoints.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/leads — create lead
GET /api/v1/leads — list with filters and pagination
GET /api/v1/leads/:id — get lead by ID (with UUID validation)
PATCH /api/v1/leads/:id — update lead (admin-only status guard)
DELETE /api/v1/leads/:id — delete lead
POST /api/v1/conversations — start conversation
GET /api/v1/conversations/:id — get conversation with messages
POST /api/v1/conversations/:id/messages — send message

steps.name: Maestro — Authentication (Signup, Login, JWT Issuance)
steps.description: User login, JWT access/refresh token issuance with JTI, refresh token rotation, bcrypt, and RBAC enforcement.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/auth/register — tenant + owner user creation
subTasks.notes: User creation done via seed; no public register endpoint yet

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/auth/login — credential check + JWT issuance
POST /api/v1/auth/refresh — refresh token rotation with new JTI
Auth middleware (JWT validation, role enum check, tenant context injection)
Password hashing with bcrypt
Role-based access control — admin-only guards on tenant creation and status patch
Structured audit logging on login success, failure, and refresh failure

steps.name: Maestro ↔ AI Worker Integration & WebSocket Streaming
steps.description: HTTP client from Maestro to AI Worker, WebSocket upgrade for real-time chat, SSE streaming proxy.

subTasks.name: HTTP client from Maestro to AI Worker with explicit timeout (resolves F-18)
WebSocket upgrade endpoint WS /ws/chat/:lead_id
SSE stream forwarding from AI Worker → WebSocket
Handoff detection and conversation state transition
Reconnect and heartbeat logic

steps.name: RAG Document Pipeline
steps.description: Document upload, chunking, embedding generation, and Qdrant vector storage for retrieval-augmented generation.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/documents — file upload to MinIO
Async chunking worker (LangChain text splitters)
Embedding generation (text-embedding-3-small)
Qdrant collection creation and vector upsert
Retrieval query at chat time (top-k similarity)
Context injection into LLM prompt

steps.name: Frontend — Chat UI & Dashboard
steps.description: Next.js auth flow, lead management table, analytics dashboard, and real-time chat UI.

subTasks.name: Next.js 16 + TypeScript strict mode project scaffold
Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui component library setup
API client with JWT refresh and localStorage token management
Authentication pages — login with form validation and password visibility toggle
Protected route middleware (Next.js Edge Runtime, redirects to /login)
WebChat conversation widget (WebSocket)
Lead management table with filters and pagination
Lead detail panel (BANT score, conversation history)
Analytics dashboard with metrics cards and status breakdown

phase2.name: Multi-Tenant Dashboard & Analytics
phase2.description: Full SaaS dashboard, tenant management, usage analytics, and team collaboration features.

phase3.name: CRM Integrations & Channel Expansion
phase3.description: Native integrations with RD Station, HubSpot, Pipefy, WhatsApp Business, and Slack notifications.

phase4.name: Enterprise Features & Fine-Tuning
phase4.description: Dedicated AI worker pools, custom model fine-tuning, advanced RBAC, SLA monitoring, and Kubernetes deployment.

positiveFindings: All repository queries use GORM parameterized placeholders — no SQL injection surface
Every query is scoped with AND tenant_id = ? — cross-tenant access blocked at DB layer
No hardcoded secrets anywhere — all config via env vars
Domain structs carry no framework imports — hexagonal isolation is real
SQL migrations are embedded in the binary — no runtime file tampering possible
Graceful shutdown with signal handling correctly implemented
Foreign key constraints with ON DELETE CASCADE enforce referential integrity
JTI claim added to all tokens (access, refresh, service) for future revocation support
Refresh token rotation implemented — new refresh token issued on every /auth/refresh call
Security response headers enforced on all routes (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy)
CORS configured with explicit allowed origins via gin-contrib/cors

audits.name: Maestro Core Security Audit — Round 1
audits.scope: Go backend — 23 source files, Steps 1 & 2 (domain, repositories, middleware, server)

findings.title: JWT_SECRET not validated on startup
Status filter value not validated before query
IDOR — missing tenant isolation in FindByConversationID
Cross-tenant message injection via IDOR on Insert
HTTP server has no read/write/idle timeouts (Slowloris)
No size limit on Message.Content
No size limit on Lead.Metadata / Tenant.Config JSONB
Health endpoint leaks environment name
No rate limiting or body-size cap on any route
IP spoofing / rate limit bypass via X-Forwarded-For
Mutex held for full map iteration in cleanupLoop
Mass assignment / tenant injection in Create
Cross-tenant IDOR via unvalidated LeadID on Conversation
Global write lock held during Allow() slow path
Mass assignment / privilege escalation in Lead Update
Unbounded message retrieval — memory exhaustion DoS
tenantIDFromContext returns empty string silently
AIWorkerURL and port not validated in config
gin.Logger() may log Authorization header values
Logger sync error silently ignored on shutdown
Unbounded memory growth in RateLimiter entries map
Incomplete domain validation (Role and ConversationStatus)
Case-sensitivity bypass in log redaction
Database DoS via unbounded offset pagination
DB connection pool missing ConnMaxLifetime / ConnMaxIdleTime
No startup retry when PostgreSQL is temporarily unavailable
No unique constraint on (tenant_id, email) for leads
No string length validation on Lead.Name, Email, Phone

audits.name: Maestro Core Security Audit — Round 2
audits.scope: Go backend — HTTP handlers, auth use case, JWT, config, middleware (Steps 3–7 additions)

findings.title: No RBAC on tenant creation — any authenticated user could create tenants
No security headers (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.)
No JTI claim — token revocation impossible
No CORS configuration — open to cross-origin requests
No refresh token rotation — stolen refresh token reusable indefinitely
Role value not validated in JWT parse — arbitrary role injection
Hardcoded bcrypt demo password in migration file
Field-level RBAC missing on PATCH /leads/:id — agents could escalate lead status
Service token lacks JTI — replay risk
Rate limiter bypassable via spoofed client IP (TRUSTED_PROXIES too wide)
Missing audit logging for sensitive auth operations
APP_ENV not validated — unexpected logging modes in prod
No UUID validation on path parameters
Request binding tags missing max-length constraints on lead fields
Demo credentials documented in plaintext in source (migration comment)
Response pagination page cap is silent — client confusion risk
DB connection pool has no explicit acquisition timeout
No explicit HTTP client for AI Worker — inherits Go default (no timeout)
Metadata JSONB validates after full parse — minor DoS surface
Unbounded memory allocation for conversation history — 500 msgs × 100 KB = 50 MB
Unhandled tenant domain collision and lack of slug normalization

patterns: JWT with JTI for future token revocation

shipped: Frontend authentication flow — login page with form validation, auth context, JWT + refresh token storage, silent refresh
Protected route middleware (Next.js Edge Runtime) — unauthenticated access redirects to /login
Maestro AuthUseCase — login with bcrypt, refresh token rotation, separate JTIs for access and refresh tokens
JWT security hardening — JTI claim on all tokens, role enum validation in Parse(), CORS via gin-contrib/cors
Security headers middleware — X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy on all routes
RBAC enforcement — admin-only guard on POST /tenants and lead status field in PATCH /leads/:id
UUID path parameter validation — uuid.Parse() guards in lead getByID and patch handlers
Audit logging — structured zap logs on login success, login failure, and refresh failure
Password visibility toggle added to login form
fixed: F-01: No RBAC on tenant creation
F-02: No security headers
F-03: No JTI claim in tokens
F-04: No CORS configuration
F-05: No refresh token rotation
F-06: Role value not validated in JWT parse
F-07: Hardcoded bcrypt hash in migration
F-08: Field-level RBAC missing on PATCH /leads/:id
F-09: Service token lacks JTI
F-10: Rate limiter bypassable via spoofed IP
F-11: Missing audit logging for auth operations
F-12: APP_ENV not validated
F-13: No UUID validation on path parameters
F-14: Max-length binding tags missing on lead fields
F-15: Demo credentials in plaintext in migration comment
F-16: Pagination page cap silent
F-17: DB pool acquisition timeout undocumented
F-19: Metadata JSONB minor DoS surface
F-22: Unbounded memory allocation (500 → 100 messages)
F-23: Tenant domain collision unhandled
DATABASE_URL password mismatch in .env
APP_ENV missing from .env
nextWeekPlanned: Maestro Step 5 — complete conversation management (GET /conversations/:id, AppendMessage, GetHistory)
Maestro Step 6 — AI Worker HTTP client (SSE streaming bridge, resolves F-18)
WhatsApp integration — Meta Cloud API webhook handler (depends on Step 6)
Maestro dashboard endpoints (GET /api/v1/dashboard/stats, conversations stats, recent leads)
